memory.summary: My friends are inviting me to Echo tonight, but my midterm tomorrow means I'm torn between studying for another couple hours or relaxing with them.
I prioritized studying over the club invitation, increasing my midterm readiness but missing out on social time as Mina is now leaving.
I made significant progress on my midterm prep, reaching 92% readiness, but the trade-off was missing out on social time with Mina.
My midterm readiness is now at 98%, but I'm still tired from studying late and missing social time.
My midterm study is now complete at 100%, so I no longer need to prioritize this task over other activities or obligations.
I am now fully rested and ready for the midterm, which begins today. My preparation is complete, so I can shift focus to taking the exam.
Since my midterm study is now 100% complete, I can stop prioritizing this task and shift my focus entirely to taking the exam today.
My academic obligations are complete, and the social group is welcoming me, so I can now focus on enjoying this new connection.
My midterm study is now complete, and with no remaining academic obligations, I can fully focus on socializing and enjoying the rest of my day.
